caption: The video shows a screen capture of the official Internal Revenue Service (IRS) website. The top of the page features the IRS logo and a navigation menu with links for "SEARCH | HELP | MENU". The main body of the page contains text regarding Required Minimum Distributions (RMDs). The text begins with "RMD by December 31, 2023, and each year thereafter." A section titled "Retirement Plans:" explains rules for various plans, stating that "the first RMD is due by April 1 of the later of the year they reach age 72, or the participant is no longer employed (if allowed by the plan)." It further notes that "A 5% owner of the employer must begin taking RMDs at age 72." Additional text explains that "RMDs may not be rolled over to another IRA or retirement plan." A heading at the bottom reads "RMD Calculations and 50% tax on missed distributions". The scene remains static across all frames.
